2 Kings 18

Hezekiah Destroys Idolatry in Judah


(2 Chronicles 29:1–2)

NoteAbi is a variant of Abijah; see 2 Chronicles 29:1.
NoteNehushtan sounds like the Hebrew for bronze and also for snake.

Sennacherib Invades Judah


(2 Chronicles 32:1–8; Psalm 46:1–11)

Note300 talents is approximately 11.3 tons or 10.3 metric tons of silver. Note30 talents is approximately 1.13 tons or 1.03 metric tons of gold.

Sennacherib Threatens Jerusalem


(2 Chronicles 32:9–19; Isaiah 36:1–22)

NoteHebrew Tartan is the title of a field marshal, general, or commander in the Assyrian military. NoteHebrew Rabsaris is the title of the chief eunuch in the Assyrian military. NoteHebrew Rabshakeh is the title of a high-ranking Assyrian military officer; here and throughout chapters 18 and 19, as well as Isaiah 36 and 37.
NoteOr in the dialect of Judah; also in verse 28
NoteOr Make a blessing with me
